skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
skill.summary: Review UI code against Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines for accessibility, UX best practices, and design compliance.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 7 potential issues (external_commands, network, weak cryptographic algorithms). Manual review confirms all findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown backticks for code formatting, not shell execution. The 'network' detection is an intentional hardcoded URL to Vercel's official GitHub guidelines repository. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are triggered by YAML frontmatter content with no actual cryptographic code. This skill is a safe design review tool that fetches official Vercel guidelines and applies them to audit UI code.

content.user_title: Review UI Code Against Design Guidelines
content.value_statement: This skill automatically audits web interfaces against Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines, identifying accessibility issues, UX problems, and design compliance violations in your UI code.
actual_capabilities: Fetch latest Vercel Web Interface Guidelines from GitHub
Review UI code files for accessibility compliance (WCAG, ARIA, color contrast)
Check UX patterns against best practices (navigation, forms, interactions)
Identify design anti-patterns and compliance violations
Output findings in structured file:line format
limitations: Requires user to specify files or patterns to review
Cannot modify or fix code - only identifies issues
Depends on external GitHub URL for guideline updates
Limited to static code analysis - cannot test runtime behavior

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audit
use_cases.description: Review a React component or HTML file for WCAG compliance, ARIA usage, and color contrast issues
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers wanting to improve accessibility

use_cases.title: Design System Compliance
use_cases.description: Check if UI components follow established design patterns and Vercel's interface guidelines

use_cases.title: Pre-launch Code Review
use_cases.description: Run a final audit on all UI files before deploying to catch any accessibility or UX issues

prompt_templates.title: Basic UI Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-design-guidelines skill to review my UI code in src/components/Button.tsx for accessibility and design compliance.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a specific component file to review

prompt_templates.title: Pattern-based Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines to audit all files matching **/*.tsx in the components directory for interface guideline violations.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to review multiple files at once

prompt_templates.title: Full Project Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run web-design-guidelines on the entire src directory to check all UI code against Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: When performing a comprehensive project review

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Review
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to review my UI code. Use web-design-guidelines and ask me which files to review.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want the skill to prompt for file selection

output: src/components/Header.tsx:15 - Missing ARIA label on navigation element
src/components/Header.tsx:23 - Color contrast ratio below 4.5:1 for link text
src/components/Header.tsx:31 - Use semantic <button> instead of <div> for actions
index.html:42 - Form labels must be associated with inputs via for attribute
index.html:58 - Images require alt text for accessibility
about.html:12 - Heading levels should not skip (h1 -> h3)

best_practices: Specify exact file paths or glob patterns to focus the review on relevant files
Review findings in file:line format and fix issues in order of severity
Fetch fresh guidelines before each review to get the latest rules
anti_patterns: Running the skill without specifying which files to review
Ignoring accessibility findings - they affect users with disabilities
Assuming the skill catches all issues - it's a helper, not a replacement for manual testing

faq.question: How does this skill fetch the guidelines?
faq.answer: It uses WebFetch to retrieve the latest rules from Vercel's official GitHub repository before each review.

faq.question: Can this skill fix the issues it finds?
faq.answer: No, the skill only identifies issues. You will need to fix them manually in your code.

faq.question: What types of issues does it check for?
faq.answer: It checks for accessibility (WCAG, ARIA), UX patterns, and general design compliance with Vercel's Web Interface Guidelines.

faq.question: Do I need an internet connection?
faq.answer: Yes, an internet connection is required to fetch the latest guidelines from GitHub.

faq.question: Can I review any file type?
faq.answer: The skill works best with HTML, JSX, TSX, and other web code formats.

faq.question: How often are the guidelines updated?
faq.answer: The guidelines are fetched fresh each time from the Vercel Labs repository, so you always get the latest rules.
